Omar Chehab

I am a third-year Ph.D. candidate at Inria, in the Mind team (formerly Parietal). I work with Aapo Hyvärinen and Alexandre Gramfort on statistical estimation of energy-based models, with an applicative interest in neuroscience.

I hold a master’s degree in applied mathematics and engineering from ENSTA Paris, and a master’s degree in machine learning from ENS Paris-Saclay.

You can reach me at l-emir-omar.chehab [AT] inria.fr


Publications

Self-Supervised Learning and Statistical Estimation

O. Chehab, A. Gramfort, A. Hyvärinen. Optimizing the Noise: from Importance Sampling to Noise-Contrastive Estimation. Submitted.

O. Chehab, A. Gramfort, A. Hyvärinen. The Optimal Noise in Noise-Contrastive Learning Is Not What You Think. Conference on Uncertainty in Artificial Intelligence (UAI), 2022.

Deep Learning and Cognitive Neuroscience

O. Chehab*, A. Defossez*, J.C. Loiseau, A. Gramfort, J.R. King. Deep Recurrent Encoder: A scalable end-to-end network to model brain signals. Journal of Neurons, Behavior, Data analysis, and Theory, 2022.

H. Banville, O. Chehab, A. Hyvärinen, D. Engemann, A. Gramfort. Uncovering the structure of clinical EEG signals with self-supervised learning. Journal of Neural Engineering, 2021.

M. Carlu*, et al. A mean-field approach to the dynamics of networks of complex neurons, from nonlinear Integrate-and-Fire to Hodgkin-Huxley models. Journal of Neurophysiology, 2020.


Teaching

I am Teacher’s Assistant (‘chargé de TD’) for the following Masters courses.

Optimization for Data Science - Institut Polytechnique de Paris (2021-2023)
Professors: Alexandre Gramfort, Pierre Ablin
Advanced Machine Learning - CentraleSupelec, Universite Paris-Saclay (2020-2022)
Professors: Emilie Chouzenoux, Frederic Pascal
Optimization - CentraleSupelec, Universite Paris-Saclay (2020-2021)
Professors: Jean-Christophe Pesquet, Sorin Olaru, Stephane Font
Last updated May 21, 2023.
